title: Delete legacy AI code (agents/scripts/rust engines) after ace solver wins
close_reason: Deleted legacy AI stack (7 agent families, training, tas_* scripts, Rust MCTS/AlphaZero/momentum engines); repo is now engine + solver.rs + solve.py + replay agents. Smoke suite green.

title: Solve L0/L5 for completeness (no human ghosts, but finish the set)
Ace solver: single Rust beam-search+polish planner to beat human ghosts
Finish remaining level solves (L4/L1/L3) after L7 deep run
notes: Forced-order experiment (L3, order 1,8,0,... = human direction): fresh completion 66.150s but zero refinement rounds (portfolio ate the 25min budget; --no-save discarded the tape). INCONCLUSIVE: fresh solves start ~equal in both directions; incumbent 58.633s advantage = 6 runs of accumulated refinement. Fair test = forced-fresh + 30min refinement under order constraint (needs a custom driver loop or sidecar-label support; solve.py only persists strictly-faster tapes). Model prefers P1-first by 0.2-0.9s; expected value of settling this ~0.2-0.5s.

title: Get A* to actually work well on at least level 7
close_reason: Abandoned: prior A* approach has failed repeatedly; resetting strategy

title: Deep L7 run: widen margin beyond 0.058s (width 300k, radius 300 corridor, long budget)
notes: Delivered far beyond scope: L7 now 19.617s validated (margin over human PR: 2.242s, was 0.058s). Achieved via time-lattice rank + selection diversity + the radius-1e9 refine hang fix.
close_reason: L7 at 19.617s validated; margin 2.242s over human PR

title: Build evidence-based superhuman ghost/TAS agent
notes: New verified L7 incumbent improved from 1334 to 1333 ticks / 22.22s in ghost_actions/L7_tas.json. Phase advances beyond 15 were tested against the current 1334 corridor: phase20 collapsed at tick 166, phase17 collapsed at tick 168, so phase15 appears near the feasible boundary for this reference corridor. A more aggressive final repair from P1@707 reached a different 1333-compatible endpoint: repair best effort missed P0 by 46.5px at segment tick 626; endpoint polish +1 COAST completed at 1334; exact final-segment local search deleted 1t from run 573 RIGHT+THR to validate 1333. Persisted validation passes: uv run python run.py --agent tas --level 7 --headless --episodes 1 --max-steps 2200 --tas-label tas --tas-validate --no-save-ghost. Focused tests pass: uv run pytest tests/test_ghost_actions.py tests/test_registries.py -q. Remaining gap vs human: agent 22.22s / 1333 ticks vs human ~21.86s / ~1311-1312 ticks; segment gaps start->P2 +0.26s, P2->P1 -0.15s, P1->P0 +0.25s. Goal still incomplete.
close_reason: Achieved verified superhuman level-7 TAS: ghost_actions/L7_tas.json validates at 1311 ticks / 21.85s, faster than stored human ghost 21.858200000000068s by 0.0082s (0.492 ticks). Evidence: TAS replay validates persisted sidecar; compare script shows P2@377 P1@690 P0@1311; focused pytest and py_compile pass.

title: L7 elite line (18-19s): needle-threading trajectory optimization (bang-bang segment solver + splice repair)
notes: 2026-07-03: L7 at 19.617s strict, validated+saved (prev AI 21.05s, human PR 21.858s). Delivered via TimeLattice time-to-go rank (V per remaining-set over cell/heading/band/posture), selection diversity (cell x speed-band round-robin), graded arc-aware doom, parabolic projection, polish macro-moves, and the radius=1e9 corridor-mark hang fix (global refines had NEVER run before). Kinematic strict floor ~19.0-19.8s; 18s flat needs the banned >316px/s skip quirk. Remaining fat: P1->P2 pendulum ~0.9s, descent slalom ~0.7s. Next: longer deep runs (anytime), finer lattice bands near pinch speeds, compound-arc templates.

title: Add a better way to have future LLM agets introspect runs so that they can debug them better
close_reason: Added scripts/inspect_run.py CLI: read-only introspection over dashboard DB + job logs + synced TB metrics. Subcommands: list/jobs/runs/job/run/errors/tail with --json. Falls back to conventional log paths when DB has stale absolute paths from another host. 9 unit tests pass. Documented in CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md so future Claude/agents discover it.
